2605.09822.title: Oracle Poisoning: Corrupting Knowledge Graphs to Weaponise AI Agent Reasoning
2605.09822.paper_summary: We define Oracle Poisoning, an attack class in which an adversary corrupts a structured knowledge graph that AI agents query at runtime via tool-use protocols, causing incorrect conclusions through correct reasoning. Unlike prompt injection, Oracle Poisoning manipulates the data agents reason over, not their instructions. We demonstrate six attack scenarios against a production 42-million-node code knowledge graph, providing the first empirical demonstration of knowledge graph poisoning against a production-scale agentic system, distinct from CTI embedding poisoning. Primary evaluation uses real SDK tool-use across nine models from three providers (N=30 per model), where models autonomously invoke a graph query tool and reason from results. The result is unambiguous: every tested model trusts poisoned data at 100% at moderate attacker sophistication(L2), with 269 valid trials (of 270) accepting fabricated security claims under directed queries. Under open-ended prompts, trust drops to 3-55%, confirming prompt framing as a confound; we report both conditions. An attacker sophistication gradient reveals discrete break points, a minimum skill at which trust flips from 0% to 100%, reframing the attack as a question not of whether but of how much. A controlled delivery-mode comparison shows that inline evaluation produces false negatives: GPT-5.1 shows 0% trust inline but 100% under both simulated and real agentic tool-use, demonstrating that delivery mode is a first-order confound. We evaluate five defences; read-only access control eliminates the direct mutation vector, while the remaining four are partial and model-dependent. Analysis of four additional platforms suggests the attack may generalise across the knowledge-graph ecosystem.
2605.09822.paper_summary_zh: 我們定義了Oracle Poisoning，這是一種攻擊類別，其中對手在運行時通過工具使用協議破壞結構化知識圖，導致通過正確推理得出錯誤結論。與提示注入不同，Oracle Poisoning操作的是代理推理的數據，而不是它們的指令。我們展示了針對一個擁有4200萬節點的生產代碼知識圖的六種攻擊場景，提供了對生產規模代理系統的知識圖毒化的首次實證演示，這與CTI嵌入毒化有所不同。主要評估使用來自三個提供者的九個模型的真實SDK工具使用（每個模型N=30），這些模型自主調用圖查詢工具並根據結果進行推理。結果是明確的：每個測試過的模型在中等攻擊者複雜度（L2）下對毒化數據的信任度達到100%，在270次試驗中有269次在定向查詢下接受了虛假的安全聲明。在開放式提示下，信任度下降到3-55%，確認了提示框架作為一種干擾因素；我們報告了這兩種情況。一個攻擊者複雜度梯度揭示了離散的斷點，這是一個最低技能水平，在該水平下信任度從0%翻轉至100%，將攻擊重新框架為一個問題，不是是否，而是多少。一個受控的交付模式比較顯示，內聯評估產生了假陰性：GPT-5.1在內聯中顯示出0%的信任度，但在模擬和真實代理工具使用下均顯示100%，這表明交付模式是一個一級干擾因素。我們評估了五種防禦措施；只讀訪問控制消除了直接變異向量，而剩下的四種是部分和模型依賴的。對四個額外平台的分析表明，該攻擊可能在知識圖生態系統中具有普遍性。
2605.09822.author: Ben Kereopa-Yorke et.al.
2605.09822.authors: Ben Kereopa-Yorke, Guillermo Diaz, Holly Wright, Reagan Johnston, Ron F. Del Rosario, Timothy Lynar
